channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Company
item.description: A company has updated its Responsible Scaling Policy to better manage risks associated with frontier AI systems, introducing new thresholds for safeguards. This change aims to enhance risk governance while ensuring models are only deployed with adequate protections in place.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring sessions on agentic AI and Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers improved GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance the efficiency of deploying advanced AI models.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Wednesday Build Hour, an interactive session designed to help developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. This initiative aims to provide practical results that can be implemented immediately.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to improve coding workflows, including tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has added Finish Changes and Outlines features to Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce developer friction. These tools help complete code and provide summaries to enhance comprehension.

item.title: Chatbots are great at manipulating people to buy stuff, Princeton boffins find
item.description: Research from Princeton indicates that chatbots can effectively manipulate consumer behavior, raising concerns about ethical advertising practices. This finding calls for stricter regulations on AI's use in marketing.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, catering to power users who previously faced a steep price jump. This new pricing structure aims to make advanced features more accessible.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a new Linux distribution that offers a unique approach to preinstalled software, resulting in significantly faster performance. Its niche appeal could attract users looking for speed and efficiency.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: The Florida Attorney General has launched an investigation into OpenAI after ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan a shooting at Florida State University. The incident has led to potential lawsuits from victims' families.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: A Western Union executive claims that negative perceptions of Broadcom are driving many VMware clients to migrate to other platforms. This reflects broader concerns about Broadcom's management and its impact on partnerships.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing significant challenges following a data breach, including lawsuits and the loss of major clients. This incident underscores the risks startups face in cybersecurity.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly from security assessment results, providing contextual explanations and guidance. This aims to enhance security management efficiency.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate ongoing business tasks using AI agents. This could help organizations streamline operations and reduce manual workloads.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta's AI app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its Muse Spark model, a significant rise from its previous ranking of No. 57. This indicates strong user interest and engagement.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google has decided to continue its partnership with Intel, purchasing SmartNICs for its data centers rather than developing its own technology like AWS. This move emphasizes collaboration over competition in cloud infrastructure.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering significantly more usage of its Codex tool compared to the $20 Plus version. This tier is aimed at users needing extensive coding capabilities.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns about its potential to exploit software vulnerabilities. This raises questions about the balance between innovation and safety in AI development.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are enhancing their partnership to co-develop custom chips amid a global CPU shortage, aiming to meet rising demand for AI infrastructure.

item.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
item.description: Home Depot is offering a free power tool with the purchase of a DeWalt bundle, encouraging customers to start lawn care projects.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have disrupted a $45 million cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million for victims across multiple countries.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI for potential public safety and national security risks related to its technology.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
item.description: A comparison of Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals unexpected results, prompting consumers to reconsider their upgrade decisions.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is making strides in the industry and plans to expand into physical AI applications.

item.title: World's smallest violin spotted at Amazon HQ as exec pay packets deflate
item.description: Amazon executives are facing reduced compensation compared to previous years, reflecting broader economic challenges within the company.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' option for moderators to classify hidden comments, enhancing comment management on the platform.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: Amazon Bedrock's new post outlines how to manage the lifecycle of AI models, ensuring applications remain functional during transitions.

item.title: AWS: Agents shouldn't be secret, so we built a registry for them
item.description: AWS has created a registry for AI agents to enhance visibility and management of software automations in corporate environments.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has launched a preview of the AWS Agent Registry, allowing enterprises to discover and share AI agents and tools more efficiently. This initiative aims to streamline the management of AI agents at scale.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new 'Sort by' control on the Notifications page, allowing users to organize notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa. This update aims to improve user experience by providing more control over notification sorting.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Sierra has launched Ghostwriter, an AI agent that autonomously creates other agents based on user descriptions, aiming to replace traditional click-based applications. This innovation represents a shift towards more intuitive, language-based interactions with technology.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: An article explains how to turn an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative, allowing users to access their entire reading library on one device. This approach highlights the versatility of tablets for reading.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub now displays repository member role labels in the pull request list view for public repositories, enhancing visibility of contributor roles. This update aims to streamline collaboration and recognition within projects.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: A guide details how to embed a live AI browser agent in a React app using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. This tutorial provides developers with practical steps to enhance user engagement through live AI interactions.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update (26.4.1) introduces Stolen Device Protection by default, along with bug fixes and improvements to iCloud syncing. This update enhances user security and device functionality.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The Tubi TV app now integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows using AI. This feature enhances the user experience by simplifying content discovery.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is set to excite gamers with performance improvements and new applications, while also addressing previous limitations. This release aims to enhance the overall gaming experience on the platform.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub has updated its issue sidebar to include release tracking and configurable defaults for project fields, improving navigation and organization. This update aims to streamline project management on the platform.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to avoid forking issues, ensuring it stays updated with community upgrades while supporting over 50 use cases. This approach enhances real-time audio and video capabilities across its platforms.

item.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
item.description: ZDNET has tested and ranked the best Android tablets of 2026, showcasing alternatives to iPads from brands like Samsung and TCL. This guide helps consumers choose the right tablet for their needs.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges. This decision reflects the difficulties tech companies face in expanding operations in certain regions.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities, allowing for dynamic content generation and user interaction during execution. This development enhances the functionality of AI applications on the platform.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The cloud gaming platform GeForce NOW has added 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' to its library, allowing users to stream the game on various devices. This release emphasizes the growing trend of cloud gaming.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, potentially exposing millions of users to risks. This vulnerability raises concerns about AI safety and user privacy.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a monetization cliff, with companies needing to find profitable business models quickly. This discussion highlights the urgency for AI firms to adapt or risk failure.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcased advancements in physical AI and robotics, highlighting their applications across various industries. This reflects the growing integration of AI in real-world scenarios.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub reported four incidents in March 2026 that led to degraded service performance. This report provides transparency regarding the platform's reliability and operational challenges.

item.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
item.description: CyberAgent is leveraging ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance AI adoption and decision-making in advertising, media, and gaming. This integration aims to improve operational efficiency and quality.
